extractions.claim: China-US air freight rate reached $5/kg in mid-Oct 2025 (TAC Index), up 2.5% WoW
extractions.evidence: TAC Index figures show average airfreight rates from China to US increased 2.5% week on week to $5 per kg as of mid-October 2025. First time rates reached $5/kg since mid-August. Rate increase driven by Trump threatening 100% tariffs on China from November 1.

verification.verifier_notes: From Air Cargo News citing TAC Index data

extractions.claim: China-US air cargo rates fell 16% to ~$6.25/kg by late December 2025
extractions.evidence: By late December 2025, China-US air cargo prices fell 16% to about $6.25/kg, their lowest level since early November. South East Asia-US rates fell 19% to $4.60/kg.

verification.verifier_notes: From Freightos weekly freight update, December 30, 2025

extractions.claim: Typical China-US air freight range in 2025: $3.10-$4.70/kg (general), $4-7/kg (spot), $8-12/kg (express/door-to-door)
extractions.evidence: In 2025, typical China-USA air freight charges per kg ranged $3.10-$4.70 depending on route, airline, season, and weight break. Spot rates $4-7/kg. Express/door-to-door rates $8-12/kg.

verification.verifier_notes: Aggregated from multiple freight forwarder rate guides

extractions.claim: Typical air freight cost per e-commerce parcel from China to US: $3-8 per parcel (0.5-2kg weight range)
extractions.evidence: Industry reports indicate cross-border e-commerce parcels (0.5-2kg) cost $3-8 to ship by air from China to US/EU, depending on route, weight, and carrier.

verification.verifier_notes: Based on per-kg rates and typical parcel weight range for e-commerce

extractions.claim: Global air cargo demand (CTK) grew 13.6% in 2024; air cargo load factor 48.3% (IATA)
extractions.evidence: Global air cargo demand (CTK) growth 2024: +13.6%. Global air cargo capacity (ACTK) growth 2024: +8.5%. Air cargo load factor 2024: 48.3%. Strong demand driven by e-commerce growth, particularly from Asia-Pacific region.

source.title: De Minimis End: Impact on Temu and Cross-Border E-Commerce 2025-2026

extractions.claim: Section 321 de minimis timeline: Feb 1 2025 EO for China → May 2 2025 China/HK exclusion → Aug 29 2025 all countries suspended → July 1 2027 permanent repeal
extractions.evidence: Feb 1, 2025: Trump EO eliminating de minimis for China imports. May 2, 2025: Section 321 eliminated for China/HK. July 30, 2025: EO 14324 suspending de minimis for all countries effective Aug 29. July 4, 2025: Congress passed 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act' permanently repealing Section 321 effective July 1, 2027.

verification.verifier_notes: From multiple legal and trade policy sources

extractions.claim: CBP processed ~4.5 million Section 321 entries per day in 2024 (95.1% of all US import entries); volume jumped from 220M in 2016 to over 1 billion
extractions.evidence: US CBP processed approximately 4,500,000 Section 321 informal entries per day in 2024. Section 321 entries represented 95.1% of all US import entries. Volume jumped from 220 million in 2016 to over a billion, with Chinese platforms accounting for ~600,000 daily shipments.

verification.verifier_notes: From CBP statistics and Red Stag Fulfillment analysis

extractions.claim: Post-de-minimis: 30% decline in Section 321 filings; processing time increased from 2-4 hours to 2-4 days; additional $10-20 brokerage/handling fees per parcel
extractions.evidence: 30% decline in total Section 321 filings. Processing times for formerly exempt shipments increased from 2-4 hours to 2-4 days. Brands must budget for new brokerage and handling fees from carriers/customs brokers, adding $10-20 to each parcel.

verification.verifier_notes: From Stord de minimis guide and industry reporting

extractions.claim: Nearly 40% of online shoppers likely to abandon carts when faced with tariff/duty surcharges at checkout
extractions.evidence: Nearly 40% of online shoppers are likely to abandon their carts when faced with extra tariff and duty surcharges at checkout.

verification.verifier_notes: From consumer survey data cited by multiple trade publications

extractions.claim: US consumer spending on Temu fell ~36% in May 2025 following de minimis end; company pivoted focus toward European markets
extractions.evidence: U.S. consumer spending on Temu fell approximately 36 percent in May 2025. The company has strategically shifted focus toward European markets.

verification.verifier_notes: From market research data cited by E-Commerce Germany News
